seoTitle: Devils River vs Devon North — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Devils River and Devon North. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Devils River vs Devon North: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Devils River and Devon North.
heading: Devils River vs Devon North — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Devils River, Victoria and Devon North, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Devon North has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Devils River (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Devils River or Devon North safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Devils River has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Devon North scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Devils River scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Devon North scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Devils River or Devon North?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Devon North has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
